summary: Completed full E2E testing of Letterboxd ZIP import feature. All 16 backend tests passed (100%) and all frontend features verified working: ZIP/CSV file upload, rating conversion (5→10), reviews as comments, watchlist import, source='letterboxd' field, LB badges on diary/watchlist/modal, and idempotent re-import.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/auth/import-letterboxd accepts .zip files
Backend: POST /api/auth/import-letterboxd still accepts .csv files (legacy)
Backend: Zip import parses ratings.csv and creates diary entries
Backend: 5-star Letterboxd ratings converted to 10-point scale (×2)
Backend: Reviews from reviews.csv merged as comments on diary entries
Backend: Watchlist.csv processed into watchlist entries
Backend: All imported diary entries have source='letterboxd'
Backend: All imported watchlist entries have source='letterboxd'
Backend: Re-import is idempotent - skips already imported items
Backend: Import returns detailed stats (diary_added, watchlist_added, skipped)
Backend: Invalid file types (.txt) rejected with 400
Backend: Import requires authentication (401 without token)
Backend: GET /api/user/watch-history returns source field on letterboxd entries
Backend: GET /api/user/watchlist returns source field on letterboxd entries
Backend: GET /api/auth/letterboxd-data returns connected=false for new user
Backend: GET /api/auth/letterboxd-data returns connected=true after import
Frontend: UserDetails Letterboxd section accepts both .zip and .csv (accept='.csv,.zip')
Frontend: Import shows stats in connected state (e.g., '4 diary entries / 2 watchlist items')
Frontend: Diary tab shows orange 'LB' badge on letterboxd-imported items
Frontend: Watchlist tab shows orange 'LB' badge on letterboxd-imported items
Frontend: Diary detail modal shows 'LB' badge on letterboxd watch entries
critical_code_review_comments: Backend ZIP parsing correctly handles root-level CSVs only (ignores deleted/orphaned/likes subfolders)
Rating conversion: Letterboxd 0-5 scale → 0-10 scale via round(rating * 2, 1)
Review lookup uses (name.lower(), year) tuple for efficient matching
Idempotency implemented by checking source='letterboxd' in existing watches array
Frontend file input correctly accepts '.csv,.zip' via accept attribute
LB badge styled as orange pill: bg-orange-500/10 border-orange-500/20 text-orange-400
Import stats displayed: diary_added, diary_updated, watchlist_added, skipped counts
test_credentials: Unique test users created per test run with timestamp-based emails
seed_data_creation: Test ZIP file at /tmp/letterboxd_test.zip with ratings.csv (Fight Club 4.5, Parasite 5, Whiplash 5), reviews.csv (Fight Club, Moonlight reviews), watchlist.csv (Eternal Sunshine, Godfather)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Letterboxd ZIP import fully tested. Test ZIP at /tmp/letterboxd_test.zip. Backend endpoint: POST /api/auth/import-letterboxd accepts multipart file upload (.zip or .csv). ZIP must contain ratings.csv, reviews.csv, watchlist.csv at root level. Ratings populate diary with 5→10 conversion. Reviews merge as comments. Watchlist items added. All imported items get source='letterboxd'. Frontend shows 'LB' badge (orange pill) on imported items in DiaryTab, WatchlistTab, and DiaryDetailModal. Connected state shows stats like '4 diary entries / 2 watchlist items'. Re-import button available for update imports. Routes: /details (import section), /my-movies (diary/watchlist tabs).
